rulings.Case Title: CONCHITA VILLAROMAN CLARK ET AL VS. RICHARD VILLAROMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Re First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 20, 2019 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANTS CONCHITA VILLAROMAN CLARK, ANGELINA HUGHES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Re First Amended Cross-Complaint DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact regarding enforceability of the contract, parties' understanding of the contract terms and course of conduct, fraud and mistake of fact. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(h)= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CONCHITA VILLAROMAN CLARK ET AL VS. RICHARD VILLAROMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Liability Issues Against Defendant Lisa Stone
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 20, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFFS CHARLES HUGHES, ANGELINA HUGHES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Liability Issues Against Defendant Lisa Stone DENIED. Motion fails to comply with CRC 3.1350 (Compare Notice of Motion, Separate Statement, and Points and Authorities). Additionally, no summary adjudication may be granted at to a part of a cause of action. "Liability" is not an issue that is allowed for adjudication under CCP 437c. Plaintiff cites no relevant authority for the proposition that summary adjudication is appropriate under the circumstances or that "damages" is not an element of any of the causes of action at issue. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CCP 437c(h).= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KUNAL MALHOTRA ET AL VS. STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 20, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Amended) DENIED. There are multiple triable issues of fact, e.g. Defendant's Facts 9-14; Plaintiff's Fact 3. The Court further notes that the moving's party arguments contained in sections 4, 5, and 6 do not contain citations to any authorities relevant to the substance of the argument. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AVALONBAY COMMUNITIES, INC. VS. LEONARD FREEMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 20, 2019 line 7. PLAINTIFF AVALONBAY COMMUNITIES' motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Hearing Required. The Court notes that neither side complied with CCP 1005: the motion was not filed and served at least 16 court days before the hearing (plus 5 days for mailing) and opposition was not served at least 9 court days before hearing. No objections to untimeliness from either side. Both parties and their counsel are ordered to comply with all CCP, CRC and Local Rules procedural provisions in future. Failure to do so will result in motions being taken off calendar or treated as unopposed. = (501/REQ)
